verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation — placing its kinase domain on the cytoplasmic face, not the extracellular face. It is therefore inner-leaflet-anchored at baseline. However, SRC satisfies the cancer-cell ecto-presentation bucket: multiple studies document SRC protein on the extracellular surface of tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase), where it is accessible to antibodies and surface-directed reagents. This oncogenic-state outer-leaflet display qualifies as `contextual` / `cell_state_induced`. The canonical biology is overwhelmingly cytoplasmic inner-leaflet, but the documented ecto-SRC pool on cancer cells is the relevant surface story for therapeutic targeting purposes.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and robustness of ecto-SRC outer-leaflet pool in tumor cells varies by study; whether wash-resistant surface display is sufficient for antibody/ADC engagement in vivo remains uncertain.
